name: Irene Reid
abstract: Irene Reid (September 23, 1930 – January 5, 2008) was an American jazz singer.

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: Irene Reid, who was with the Basie band as a singer and has become a very good friend of mine for a long time
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Irene Reid, I had all the singing gigs from Birdland so consequently I was always on the scene.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Irene Reid was the featured singer. I can’t remember. I can see the drummer, but I can’t remember his name. ... I went to the Blue Morocco and sat in. A couple of weeks after I had sat in, had been up there, Irene Reid broke her ankle, and they called me to replace her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Irene Reid, but the interviewee (Maxine Sullivan) does not respond or provide any personal connection or interaction details regarding Irene Reid.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Irene Reid was one of the people. ... Irene Reid did a big interview which was carried in the New York Times about how the Jazz Foundation has helped her
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: I did the writing for an Irene Reid recording session that was done with a big band
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 7, 1998
